  "findings": [
    "Nails placed too high, outside the designated common bond/fastening line.",
    "Exposed nail heads visible on the shingle surface, creating direct pathways for water intrusion."
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Remove affected shingles and replace them using correct nail placement within the manufacturer's specified fastening zone.",
    "Seal any exposed underlayment penetrations before installing replacement shingles."
  ],
  "rationale": "High or exposed nail placement violates both IRC R905 fastening requirements and manufacturer installation specifications. High nailing misses the double-coverage common bond area, reducing wind resistance, while exposed nails lead to premature leaks and water damage.",

    "IRC R905.2.5",
